title: Validate
description: The #[Validate] attribute associates validation rules with component properties, enabling automatic real-time validation and clean rule declaration.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
Apply the #[Validate] attribute to properties that need validation:
With #[Validate], Livewire automatically validates properties on each update, providing instant feedback to users.

examples.code:
validate();  Post::create([ 'title' => $this->title, 'content' => $this->content, ]);  return redirect('/posts'); }};?> 
@error('title') {{ $message }} @enderror  @error('content') {{ $message }} @enderror

sections.title: #How it works
sections.content: When you add #[Validate] to a property:

sections.title: #Real-time validation
sections.content:
By default, #[Validate] validates properties as they're updated:
As users fill out the form, they receive immediate validation feedback.

examples.code: @error('email') {{ $message }} @enderror  @error('password') {{ $message }} @enderror

sections.title: #Disabling auto-validation
sections.content:
To validate only when explicitly calling $this->validate(), use onUpdate: false:
Now validation only runs when save() is called, not on every property update.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/post/⚡create.blade.php use Livewire\Attributes\Validate;use Livewire\Component;use App\Models\Post; new class extends Component { #[Validate('required|min:3', onUpdate: false)] public $title = '';  #[Validate('required|min:3', onUpdate: false)] public $content = '';  public function save() { $validated = $this->validate(); Post::create($validated); return redirect('/posts'); }};

sections.title: #Custom attribute names
sections.content:
Customize the field name in validation messages:
Error message will be "The date of birth field is required" instead of "The dob field is required".

sections.title: #Custom validation messages
sections.content:
Override default validation messages:
For multiple rules, use multiple attributes:

examples.code: #[Validate('required', message: 'Please provide a post title')] public $title;
#[Validate('required', message: 'Please provide a post title')]#[Validate('min:3', message: 'This title is too short')]public $title;

sections.title: #Array validation
sections.content:
Validate array properties and their children:
This validates both the array itself and each individual task.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/⚡task-list.blade.php use Livewire\Attributes\Validate;use Livewire\Component; new class extends Component { #[Validate([ 'tasks' => 'required|array|min:1', 'tasks.*' => 'required|string|min:3', ])] public $tasks = [];  public function addTask() { $this->tasks[] = ''; }};

sections.title: #Limitations
sections.content: PHP attributes can't use Laravel's Rule objects directly. For complex rules like Rule::exists(), use a rules() method instead:

sections.title: #When to use
sections.content:
Use #[Validate] when:
Use rules() method when:

sections.title: #Example: Contact form
sections.content:
Here's a complete contact form with validation:
Users get immediate feedback as they fill out the form, with friendly field names and helpful error messages.

examples.code:
validate();  Mail::to('[email protected]')->send(new ContactMessage($validated));  session()->flash('success', 'Message sent successfully!');  $this->reset(); }};?> 
@if (session('success'))
{{ session('success') }}
@endif 
@error('name') {{ $message }} @enderror
 
@error('email') {{ $message }} @enderror
 
@error('subject') {{ $message }} @enderror
 
@error('message') {{ $message }} @enderror

sections.title: #Learn more
sections.content: For comprehensive documentation on validation, including form objects, custom rules, and testing, see the Validation documentation.

examples.code: #[Validate( mixed $rule = null, ?string $attribute = null, ?string $as = null, mixed $message = null, bool $onUpdate = true, bool $translate = true,)]
